title: Completed action with 了 (le)
explanation: 了 (le) placed after a verb indicates a completed action. It focuses on the fact that something has been done.

examples.pinyin: Tā mǎi le yì běn shū.

title: Experience with 过 (guò)
explanation: 过 (guò) after a verb indicates that someone has had the experience of doing something at some point in the past.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ chī guò Běijīng kǎoyā ma?
examples.english: Have you ever eaten Peking duck?

title: Progressive with 正在 (zhèngzài)
explanation: 正在 (zhèngzài) indicates an action is in progress right now, similar to the English present continuous tense.

examples.english: He is reading a book right now.

title: Comparison with 比 (bǐ)
explanation: 比 (bǐ) is used to compare two things. A is [more adjective] than B.

title: From...to with 从...到 (cóng...dào)
explanation: 从...到 expresses a range of time or place, meaning "from...to".

examples.chinese: 从北京到上海要五个小时。
examples.pinyin: Cóng Běijīng dào Shànghǎi yào wǔ gè xiǎoshí.
examples.english: It takes five hours from Beijing to Shanghai.

examples.chinese: 我从九点到十二点上课。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng jiǔ diǎn dào shí'èr diǎn shàng kè.

title: About to happen with 要...了 (yào...le)
explanation: 要...了 indicates something is about to happen soon.

examples.english: The train is about to depart.

title: Ability with 可以, 能, 会
explanation: 可以 expresses permission, 能 expresses ability or possibility, and 会 expresses a learned skill. All translate roughly to "can".

title: Complement of degree with 得 (de)
explanation: 得 (de) links a verb to a description of how the action is performed.

title: 还是 vs 或者 (háishi vs huòzhě)
explanation: 还是 is used in questions offering a choice. 或者 is used in statements meaning "or".

examples.english: Do you drink coffee or tea? (question)

examples.chinese: 周末我看书或者看电影。
examples.pinyin: Zhōumò wǒ kàn shū huòzhě kàn diànyǐng.
examples.english: On weekends I read or watch movies. (statement)

title: 一点儿 vs 有点儿 (yìdiǎnr vs yǒudiǎnr)
structure: Verb + 一点儿 / 有点儿 + Adj
explanation: 一点儿 means "a little" and modifies nouns or follows adjectives in comparisons. 有点儿 means "somewhat" and is used before adjectives, often with a negative nuance.
